rationale: The citing paper is an eye-tracking study linking visual attention to recall/purchase outcomes, and the cited paper is also an eye-tracking paper on how attention maps to choice processes, so there is plausible methodological/conceptual relevance. However, the provided evidence includes only a single DOI-matched reference with no in-text citation context, and the domains differ (online news ads vs. conjoint tasks), so it is not safe to classify as clearly substantive or clearly incidental.
